skill.name: pptx-generation
skill.description: Create professional PowerPoint decks with structured planning, html2pptx workflow guidance, accessibility checks, and validation procedures for simple or complex presentations.
skill.summary: Generate polished PowerPoint decks with planned layouts, brand-aware design, and validation gates.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk weak-cryptography and external-command patterns, but review found Markdown documentation, prompt templates, file references, and GraphViz labels rather than executable code. No prompt injection, credential access, data exfiltration, or malicious behavior was found in the reviewed skill files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the reviewed locations are Markdown code fences and inline documentation examples. They describe presentation prompts and reference files; they do not execute commands or pass user input to a shell.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are visible Markdown fences or inline code references. No executable script, shell invocation, or dynamic command construction appears at these locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matches Presentation Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts appear to match ordinary presentation terminology, Markdown filenames, and design text. Review found no cryptographic functions, hashing calls, credential handling, or security-sensitive algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed context contains presentation guidance and diagram labels, not cryptographic code. The files are documentation assets without executable implementations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hardcoded URL Is a Documentation Reference
low_findings.description: The only network finding is a comment in the GraphViz workflow diagram that cites an external article. It is not fetched by the skill and does not transmit local data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears in a comment line inside a DOT file. No network API, downloader, or runtime path uses the URL.

content.value_statement: High-quality slide decks are difficult because they combine analysis, narrative, layout, and accessibility requirements. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured workflow for planning, generating, and validating PowerPoint presentations.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of simple PowerPoint decks up to 15 slides with a single prompt template.
Defines a multi-chat architecture for complex decks of 30 or more slides.
Requires pre-execution design plans with layout, color, typography, and emphasis rules.
Documents accessibility checks for contrast, font size, margins, overlap, and bullet count.
Provides brand integration guidance for adapting colors, fonts, spacing, and visual rules.
Includes a GraphViz workflow diagram for the simple and complex deck process.
limitations: It provides workflow instructions and reference templates, not a standalone PowerPoint rendering engine.
It assumes an html2pptx workflow is available in the user environment.
It does not replace human review for data accuracy, executive messaging, or brand approval.
Very complex decks may still require section-by-section iteration and manual assembly checks.

use_cases.title: Executive Board Deck
use_cases.description: Create a polished presentation from financial data, memos, and key decisions with clear narrative flow.

use_cases.title: Branded Client Presentation
use_cases.description: Apply brand colors, typography, spacing, and accessibility rules while avoiding fragile layout patterns.

use_cases.title: Large Analysis Deck
use_cases.description: Split a long presentation into architect, generator, and assembly phases for better consistency.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Deck
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a short presentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a PowerPoint deck of up to 10 slides about [topic]. Use the simple deck workflow, write a design plan first, then generate slides with accessibility checks.

prompt_templates.title: Turn Data Into Slides
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request using source files or analysis notes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these source materials: [files or notes]. Build a presentation for [audience] with a clear story, key insights, and a validation checklist before delivery.

prompt_templates.title: Apply Brand Guidelines
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for branded presentation output.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use these brand guidelines: [brand details]. Extract colors, fonts, spacing, and constraints, then create a brand-adapted PowerPoint design plan and deck.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Complex Deck
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a long executive presentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a [number]-slide executive deck using the complex deck architecture. Create the narrative structure, slide outline, master design plan, generator instructions, and assembly validation steps.

output_examples.input: A team needs a 12-slide quarterly business review from finance notes and customer metrics.
output_examples.output: A planned PowerPoint deck with an executive summary, metric slides, risk analysis, recommendations, and validation notes for contrast, margins, and readability.

output_examples.input: A consultant needs a branded strategy presentation using client colors and typography.
output_examples.output: A brand-adapted deck plan that converts brand rules into accessible slide layouts, color usage, typography hierarchy, and slide-by-slide requirements.

output_examples.input: An analyst needs a 35-slide market analysis presentation from several source documents.
output_examples.output: A multi-phase plan with architect outputs, section generator instructions, assembly checks, and consistency validation across the full presentation.

best_practices: Start with the design plan before generating any slide content.
Use the simple workflow for short decks and the multi-chat architecture for long decks.
Run the validation checklist before considering the presentation complete.
anti_patterns: Do not generate a full complex deck in one pass without a master design plan.
Do not use bordered text boxes, outline shapes, or rounded rectangles with text.
Do not skip contrast, font size, margin, and overlap checks.

faq.question: Does this skill generate PowerPoint files directly?
faq.answer: It provides the workflow, prompts, and validation steps for generating PowerPoint decks. It expects a compatible html2pptx workflow.

faq.question: When should I use the complex deck architecture?
faq.answer: Use it for presentations of 30 or more slides, or when many visual elements may exceed a single context window.

faq.question: Can it follow brand guidelines?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a process for extracting brand colors, typography, spacing, and constraints from reference materials.

faq.question: How does it improve slide quality?
faq.answer: It requires planning, layout constraints, accessibility checks, and validation gates before final delivery.

faq.question: Is it suitable for executive presentations?
faq.answer: Yes. The templates are designed for board decks, reports, strategic analysis, and professional stakeholder presentations.

faq.question: What are the main accessibility checks?
faq.answer: The checklist covers contrast ratios, font sizes, margins, overlapping elements, bullet count, and readability at thumbnail size.
